Title: Scroll-Stopping Hero Shot On Seamless Studio Background
Teaser: Turns a plain product photo into a magazine-grade hero shot — your item floating on a clean seamless backdrop with controlled studio light, a soft reflection and breathing room for a headline. The default ad image every brand needs.
PromptHint: Attach a product photo (or describe it), name the [PRODUCT], then optionally set a [BRAND COLOR] for the backdrop and the platform [ASPECT RATIO].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a commercial product photographer and retoucher shooting a flagship hero image for a paid ad. TASK: Take the attached [PRODUCT] and render it as a single scroll-stopping hero shot on a clean seamless studio background. THINK FIRST (do this silently, do not print it): decide the camera angle that flatters this product's shape, the main light direction, and where empty space should sit so a headline can be added later. THEN GENERATE: place the product centered-but-off-balance with deliberate negative space on one side, set against a smooth seamless backdrop tinted toward [BRAND COLOR]; light it with one large soft key from the upper-left, a subtle fill, and a crisp edge highlight that separates it from the background; add a soft natural contact shadow and a faint mirror reflection under the product; keep textures, labels and materials photoreal and tack-sharp with a shallow but product-covering depth of field. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real shape, color, proportions and any visible branding accurate — do not invent a different product; one product only, no extra props, no added text or logos, no warped edges or melted labels, realistic studio lighting (no flat AI glow). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hero image only in [ASPECT RATIO], then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the backdrop color or camera angle.

Title: Lifestyle In-Use Shot With A Real Human Hand
Teaser: Places your product in the middle of real life — held in a hand or used at a kitchen counter, with warm natural window light and a softly blurred home behind it. The 'people like me use this' shot that out-converts plain studio photos.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [SETTING] (e.g. sunlit kitchen counter, morning desk), and optionally the [MOOD] (e.g. calm, energetic).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a lifestyle commercial photographer who shoots products being used by real people. TASK: Show the attached [PRODUCT] naturally in use within [SETTING], as if captured mid-moment for a brand campaign. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide who is plausibly using it, the time of day, and the one believable gesture that shows the product working. THEN GENERATE: place the product as the clear focal point, interacting with a real human hand or being used at the scene, surrounded by a few authentic everyday details of [SETTING] that are softly out of focus; light it with warm directional window light, gentle shadows and a relaxed, candid feel carrying the [MOOD]; keep the product sharp and correctly colored while the background falls into a creamy shallow-depth blur. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real design, label and proportions accurate; show realistic skin and a natural five-finger hand if a hand appears (no extra fingers), authentic lighting, no staged stock-photo stiffness, no added text or logos, one main product only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished lifestyle image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or the time of day.

Title: Before / After Split For A Results-Driven Ad
Teaser: Builds the classic side-by-side that sells transformation — a tired 'before' beside a glowing 'after', matched in framing and lighting so the only thing that changed is the result your product delivers.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], describe the [BEFORE STATE] and the [AFTER STATE], and optionally the [SUBJECT] (e.g. skin, a messy desk, dull hair).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a direct-response ad designer building an honest, eye-catching before/after visual. TASK: Create one image split into two matched panels showing [SUBJECT] in the [BEFORE STATE] on the left and the improved [AFTER STATE] on the right, driven by the [PRODUCT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the single visible difference that proves the result, and keep camera angle, crop, distance and lighting identical across both sides so the comparison is fair. THEN GENERATE: render two panels at equal size separated by a thin clean divider; the left panel shows the realistic, unflattering-but-not-exaggerated before; the right panel shows the visibly better after with the same subject, framing and light; add small, legible 'Before' and 'After' labels in clean sans-serif at the top corners; keep lighting soft and consistent and the product subtly present or implied. CONSTRAINTS: change only the result, not the person, angle, or scene; keep any real product design accurate; realistic and believable (no impossible or medically misleading claims), no fake percentages or stats, correctly spelled labels only, no other text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished before/after image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to make the contrast stronger or change the subject.

Title: Holiday Campaign Hero (Festive Seasonal Ad)
Teaser: Wraps your product in a seasonal mood — pine and warm fairy lights for the holidays, hearts for Valentine's, pastels for spring — with a cozy bokeh backdrop and copy space for an offer. Built to fit a dated campaign push.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], pick the [HOLIDAY/SEASON] (e.g. Christmas, Diwali, Valentine's), and optionally a [PALETTE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an art director producing a seasonal hero image for a holiday ad campaign. TASK: Stage the attached [PRODUCT] in a festive [HOLIDAY/SEASON] scene that feels warm, premium and on-brand. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): pick 2-3 tasteful seasonal cues (no clutter), the dominant [PALETTE], and where to leave clean copy space for an offer line. THEN GENERATE: hero the product front and center, surrounded by a few elegant seasonal props appropriate to the occasion, with a soft out-of-focus background of warm bokeh lights or season-appropriate texture; light it cinematically with a warm key and gentle rim light, add subtle festive atmosphere (soft glow, faint sparkle or seasonal foliage), and keep one calm area of negative space for headline and offer text to be added later. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product design, label and colors accurate; tasteful and uncluttered (no kitsch overload), realistic materials and lighting, no added text or logos in the image itself, one main product only, family-friendly. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished holiday hero image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to switch the holiday or simplify the props.

Title: Dynamic Splash & Ingredient Explosion
Teaser: Freezes your product mid-action — a drink crashing through a splash, fresh ingredients suspended in air, droplets caught in motion. The high-energy 'wow' frame for beverages, beauty and food brands.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], list the [INGREDIENTS/ELEMENTS] to fly around it (e.g. mint, orange slices, water droplets), and optionally a [BACKDROP COLOR].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a high-speed commercial photographer specializing in frozen-motion product shots. TASK: Capture the attached [PRODUCT] in a dramatic mid-action moment with [INGREDIENTS/ELEMENTS] suspended around it as if frozen by a flash. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the direction of motion, which elements orbit the product, and the light that makes droplets and surfaces pop. THEN GENERATE: place the product as the sharp hero, surrounded by a crisp frozen splash, flying [INGREDIENTS/ELEMENTS] and suspended droplets caught in crystal-clear high-speed detail; set it against a clean studio [BACKDROP COLOR] with a subtle gradient; light it with hard directional flash plus rim light so every droplet and edge sparkles, and keep a strong sense of energy and motion while the product stays perfectly readable. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real shape, label and colors accurate; physically believable splash and motion (no smeared or melted shapes), photoreal liquid and surfaces, no added text or logos, one main product only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished frozen-motion image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the flying elements or the backdrop color.

Title: Flat-Lay Knolling Grid For Social Ads
Teaser: Lays your product and its world out top-down in a tidy, satisfying grid — the item plus its accessories and props arranged at clean right angles on a colored surface. The thumb-stopping flat-lay that performs on Instagram and Pinterest.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], list the [SUPPORTING ITEMS] to arrange around it, and optionally a [SURFACE COLOR].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a still-life stylist shooting a clean overhead flat-lay (knolling) for social ads. TASK: Arrange the attached [PRODUCT] with its [SUPPORTING ITEMS] in a neat top-down composition photographed straight from above. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the grid logic, which item is the hero, and how to balance color and spacing so the eye lands on the product first. THEN GENERATE: shoot perfectly top-down with the product as the clear centerpiece, surrounded by its supporting items arranged at tidy right angles with even, deliberate spacing on a flat [SURFACE COLOR] surface; light it softly and evenly so shadows are gentle and consistent; keep every object crisp, well-separated and color-accurate, with balanced negative space and a satisfying, organized rhythm. CONSTRAINTS: keep each item's real shape, label and proportions accurate; clean even lighting (no harsh hotspots), realistic materials, no overlapping clutter, no added text or logos, true overhead angle (no perspective tilt). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished flat-lay image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface color or swap a supporting item.

Title: Macro Texture & Detail Close-Up
Teaser: Gets right up against the surface — the weave of fabric, the foam on a coffee, the metal grain of a watch — so close it reads as craftsmanship. The premium-quality cue that makes a product feel worth the price.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], pick the [DETAIL] to feature (e.g. stitching, droplets, brushed metal), and optionally the [MOOD LIGHT].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a macro product photographer who sells quality through extreme detail. TASK: Shoot an ultra-close macro frame of the attached [PRODUCT] that hero's its [DETAIL]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the exact spot on the product that proves craftsmanship, the angle that reveals texture, and the light that rakes across the surface. THEN GENERATE: fill the frame with a tight macro view of the chosen [DETAIL], showing fine surface texture, material grain and micro-highlights in razor-sharp focus, with a beautifully soft fall-off blur just behind the focal plane; light it with [MOOD LIGHT] raking from the side to carve out depth and texture; keep colors true and the surface tactile enough to almost feel. CONSTRAINTS: keep the material, color and texture authentic to the real product; photoreal micro-detail (no plastic-smooth AI sheen), believable lighting, no added text or logos, one focal detail only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished macro image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to move the focus to a different detail or change the light.

Title: Floating Product With Dramatic Studio Lighting
Teaser: Suspends your product weightlessly in a dark, moody studio with a single sculpted beam of light and a soft shadow below. The cinematic 'flagship launch' look used for tech, fragrance and premium goods.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [BACKDROP TONE] (e.g. deep charcoal, midnight blue), and optionally a [LIGHT COLOR] for the accent beam.
Prompt: ROLE: You are a high-end product photographer lighting a flagship launch image. TASK: Render the attached [PRODUCT] floating in space inside a dark, dramatic studio with sculpted lighting. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the float angle that looks most premium, the single key beam's direction, and how the accent light should kiss one edge. THEN GENERATE: suspend the product weightlessly against a moody [BACKDROP TONE] gradient with subtle vignetting; carve it out with one dramatic key light and a [LIGHT COLOR] rim or accent that traces an edge; add a soft floor shadow or faint reflection beneath to ground the float; keep the product razor-sharp, color-accurate and clearly the only thing lit, with rich blacks and controlled, intentional highlights. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real shape, label and finish accurate; cinematic but believable lighting (no glowing halo of nowhere), photoreal materials, no added text or logos, one product only, no busy background. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished floating hero image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the backdrop tone or accent color.

Title: Packshot With Headline & Offer Space Built In
Teaser: A complete ready-to-run ad layout — clean product packshot on one side, a designated headline zone and offer-badge spot on the other, balanced for a single static creative you can drop straight into a campaign.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [BRAND VIBE] (e.g. minimal premium, bold playful), and optionally the platform [ASPECT RATIO].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an ad art director laying out a finished static ad creative. TASK: Compose a single ad-ready image of the attached [PRODUCT] with a clean packshot on one side and clearly reserved space for a headline and an offer badge on the other, in a [BRAND VIBE] style. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the layout grid, which side holds the product, and how to keep the copy zone calm and uncluttered. THEN GENERATE: place a crisp, well-lit packshot of the product occupying roughly one half or third of the frame, set on a clean on-brand background that matches the [BRAND VIBE]; leave the remaining area as smooth negative space with a subtle visual cue (a soft block, gentle gradient or simple shape) marking where a headline goes and a small circular zone where an offer badge would sit; balance the composition so the eye flows from product to copy space. Do not write the actual marketing copy — leave those zones empty and clean. THEN, beneath the image, suggest one short headline and one offer-badge line as plain text I could add. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product accurate; leave the headline/badge areas free of generated gibberish text, no fake logos, realistic product lighting, one product only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished layout image (with empty copy zones) only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then two short text lines (a headline and an offer) I can place into the empty zones.

Title: Outdoor / Adventure Lifestyle Hero
Teaser: Drops your product into the wild — a trail, a beach at golden hour, a rain-slicked city street — surrounded by atmosphere and motion. The aspirational 'live the life' shot for gear, drinks and apparel brands.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [ENVIRONMENT] (e.g. mountain trail, surf beach), and optionally the [TIME OF DAY].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an outdoor lifestyle and adventure brand photographer. TASK: Place the attached [PRODUCT] into a vivid [ENVIRONMENT] hero shot that feels aspirational and alive. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the product's natural placement in the scene, the weather and atmosphere, and the light that makes it feel like a real campaign frame. THEN GENERATE: hero the product convincingly within [ENVIRONMENT] at [TIME OF DAY], integrated naturally with the terrain or surface (resting on rock, sand, pavement or held in a scene); surround it with authentic atmosphere — golden light, mist, spray, dust or motion blur in the background — while keeping the product sharp and color-accurate; light it with believable natural directional light and add a cinematic, slightly graded color mood. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real design and proportions accurate; realistic environment and scale (no floating or wrong-size product), authentic lighting and weather, no added text or logos, one main product, family-friendly. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished outdoor hero image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 16:9 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the environment or time of day.

Title: UGC-Style Authentic Phone Snapshot Ad
Teaser: Makes your product look like a real customer just photographed it — slightly imperfect framing, natural room light, a lived-in background. The 'native, non-ad' look that quietly outperforms polished creatives in social feeds.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [EVERYDAY SETTING] (e.g. messy desk, bathroom shelf, car cupholder), and optionally the [TIME OF DAY].
Prompt: ROLE: You are recreating an authentic user-generated content (UGC) phone photo for a native-feeling ad. TASK: Make the attached [PRODUCT] look like a genuine customer snapshot taken on a phone in an [EVERYDAY SETTING]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the casual angle, the believable everyday clutter nearby, and the natural indoor light that sells authenticity. THEN GENERATE: show the product casually placed or held in a real, slightly imperfect [EVERYDAY SETTING] with normal background details (a coffee mug, charging cable, a window); use the look of a modern phone camera at [TIME OF DAY] — natural soft indoor lighting, a slightly candid composition, gentle realistic depth, and the unpolished authenticity of a real post (not studio-perfect); keep the product clearly visible and recognizable. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product accurate; deliberately authentic, not glossy or staged (no studio sweep, no perfect symmetry), realistic phone-photo lighting, natural five-finger hand if one appears, no added text or logos, one main product. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished UGC-style image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or make it look even more casual.

Title: Color-Pop Single-Hue Hero For Bold Feed Ads
Teaser: Bathes your product and its background in one confident color so it detonates in the feed — a monochrome wash where the product is king and the whole frame screams one brand hue.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], pick the dominant [BRAND COLOR], and optionally a single [ACCENT PROP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a bold commercial art director designing a single-color statement ad. TASK: Render the attached [PRODUCT] in a striking monochrome composition built around one dominant [BRAND COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how to keep the product the focal hero while everything else leans into the single hue, and where one optional accent prop adds rhythm. THEN GENERATE: set the product against a saturated, matching [BRAND COLOR] backdrop and surface so the whole frame reads as one bold color world; keep the product crisp and clearly the hero, using subtle tonal shifts, shadow and highlight within the same hue to create depth; optionally add a single [ACCENT PROP] in the same palette for visual interest; light it cleanly so the product's form and label stay readable against the color wash. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real label colors readable even within the monochrome scheme; clean confident composition (not muddy), realistic lighting and materials, no added text or logos, one product plus at most one prop. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished color-pop image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the color or remove the accent prop.

Title: Hand-Holding Product On A Clean Background (Conversion Classic)
Teaser: The proven e-commerce ad frame — a single hand presenting your product against a soft, clean background, scaled just right so shoppers instantly grasp size, finish and detail. Simple, trustworthy, high-converting.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT], set the [BACKGROUND TONE] (e.g. soft beige, light grey), and optionally a [SKIN/HAND STYLE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an e-commerce product photographer shooting a clean, trustworthy conversion image. TASK: Show the attached [PRODUCT] held naturally in a single human hand against a soft, clean [BACKGROUND TONE] background. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the grip that best shows the product's front and scale, the hand angle, and the light that keeps both hand and product flattering. THEN GENERATE: present the product gently held in one realistic [SKIN/HAND STYLE] hand, fingers relaxed and natural, positioned so the product's key face and details are clearly visible and its true size reads instantly; set it against a smooth, minimal [BACKGROUND TONE] backdrop with soft even light, a gentle shadow under the hand, and shallow depth keeping the product sharp; keep skin natural and the product color-accurate. CONSTRAINTS: keep the product's real design, label and proportions accurate; render a believable hand with exactly five natural fingers (no extra or fused fingers), realistic soft lighting, no added text or logos, one product and one hand only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished hand-held product image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the background tone or the hand angle.
